metadata.description: Comprehensive pre-submission verification of NatCol manuscript via parallel subagents auditing each major section (abstract/intro, methods, results, discussion/conclusion, figures/tables/numerical consistency, voice/venue-fit). Aggregate into per-metric scores and overall confidence for Nature Collection (PALCOMMS) submission.

summary:
6-agent parallel audit of NatCol manuscript completed. Headline findings: manuscript is publishable but has ~15 addressable issues concentrated in three areas — (a) Methods reproducibility gaps (MLP training, k=20 selection criteria, Robustness Checks subsection under-specified), (b) abstract/intro numerical inconsistency (abstract says "two of twenty" but canonical is "four of twenty"), and (c) theoretical-citation fit (Latour 1992 misapplied at L311; Bowker & Star 1999 gestured at L307 without naming which concept; Post-Mediatized Sphere subsection is weakest). Overall confidence: 7.8/10 as-is, 8.5/10 after the 15 fixes. Strongest dimensions: PALCOMMS venue fit (9/10), numerical consistency (9/10), novelty (8.5/10). Weakest: Methods reproducibility (6.5/10), pre-submission readiness (6.5/10), theoretical grounding (7/10).

WHAT IS NEXT:
1. Decide whether to execute the 15 prioritised fixes (critical + medium tier). I have them enumerated.
2. Verify the Baeyaert 2025 citation directly (4 uses; DOI was 403-blocked last session); if it doesn't actually frame "marginalisation without explicit exclusion" as attributed, find a better anchor.
3. Verify Latour (1992) misapplication finding at L311; consider Bourdieu porte-parole or Fraser subaltern counterpublics as replacement, or rewrite without theoretical label.
4. Fix grammatical residue at L325 ("responsibility attributions ... topic into oppositional networks" → "resolve into" or "fall into").
5. Fix 5 remaining US-spelling slips outside bibliography (personalization L98, unauthorized L25+L349, editorialized L100+L367).
6. Fix abstract "two of twenty" → "four of twenty".
7. Expand Methods for MLP training procedure, k=20 selection criteria in-text, and Robustness Checks statistical test naming.
8. Cut or shorten Results Summary block (L263-269) which is the most AI-shaped scaffold per Agent 6.
9. Consider cutting/shortening Post-Mediatized Sphere subsection (L341-345) per Agent 4.
10. Deadline April 30, 2026 (16 days).

contribution.description: Ran 6 parallel verification subagents auditing distinct parts of NatCol manuscript: (1) Abstract+Introduction, (2) Methods, (3) Results, (4) Discussion+Conclusion, (5) Figures/Tables/Numerical consistency, (6) Voice/PALCOMMS venue fit. Each agent returned structured findings (strengths, critical/medium/minor concerns, fixes, section scores). Aggregated into per-metric scores across 9 submission-quality dimensions and produced overall confidence rating for Nature Collection (PALCOMMS) submission.

context.conversation_snippet: "do another verification round and run a subagent for each part of the manuscript that you can to test that it is properly used, properly cited (if applicable), properly referenced, adds value to the manuscript, etc. give me your confidence in the manuscript at the end and also how it scores across a few metrics"
